title: Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice

seo.title: Patbingsu Recipe: Authentic Korean Shaved Ice Dessert
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Patbingsu (Korean red bean shaved ice) at home! This easy recipe brings the refreshing taste of Korea to your kitchen. Perfect for summer.
lowCompetitionKeywords: Korean red bean shaved ice recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Patbingsu (Korean Red Bean Shaved Ice) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Patbingsu: Step-by-Step Guide
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of homemade Patbingsu, Korean red bean shaved ice, topped with sweet red beans, mochi, and condensed milk.
seo.imageCaption: Cool down with a refreshing bowl of Patbingsu, a classic Korean shaved ice dessert perfect for any hot day!

description: Patbingsu is a beloved Korean dessert that offers a refreshing escape from the heat, featuring finely shaved ice generously topped with sweet red beans. This iconic treat combines a delightful medley of textures and flavors, from the creamy condensed milk to chewy rice cakes and fresh fruit. It's a perfect blend of sweetness and coolness, making it an irresistible indulgence on any warm day.

ingredients.notes: or to taste, for red beans
or mini mochi, cut into bite-sized pieces

instructions.title: Prepare Sweet Red Beans (Pat)
instructions.text: Rinse the dried red beans thoroughly. Soak them in plenty of water overnight, or for at least 8 hours. Drain the soaking water. Place the soaked beans in a pot with fresh water, bring to a boil, then simmer for 10 minutes. Drain this water. Add fresh water again, ensuring the beans are covered by about 2 inches of water, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 30-45 minutes, or until the beans are very tender. Drain most of the water, leaving just enough to prevent sticking. Add granulated sugar and mash some of the beans with a spoon or potato masher to create a slightly chunky paste. Simmer for another 5-10 minutes until thickened. Let cool completely.

instructions.title: Prepare Milk Ice
instructions.text: Pour the whole milk into shallow freezer-safe containers or ice cube trays. For best results, use shallow dishes so the milk freezes thinly and is easier to shave. Freeze solid for at least 4-6 hours, or preferably overnight.

instructions.title: Prepare Toppings
instructions.text: While the milk is freezing, prepare your fresh fruit toppings. Slice the strawberries, kiwi, and banana into bite-sized pieces. If using plain tteok (rice cakes), boil them briefly in water until soft, then drain and cool. If using pre-made mochi, simply cut into desired sizes.

instructions.title: Shave the Ice
instructions.text: Once the milk is completely frozen, use a bingsu machine, a food processor with a shaving attachment, or a sturdy blender capable of crushing ice to shave the frozen milk into fine, fluffy flakes. If using a blender, pulse until you achieve a snow-like consistency, being careful not to over-blend into a liquid.

instructions.title: Assemble Patbingsu
instructions.text: Divide the finely shaved milk ice evenly among individual serving bowls. Mound the ice gently in the center of each bowl.

instructions.title: Arrange Toppings and Serve
instructions.text: Generously spoon the prepared sweet red beans (pat) over the shaved ice in each bowl. Artfully arrange the sliced fresh fruits and cut mochi pieces on top of the red beans and ice. Drizzle a generous amount of sweetened condensed milk over all the toppings. Serve immediately to enjoy the refreshing coolness before the ice melts.

tips: For the fluffiest ice, freeze milk in shallow trays and use a dedicated bingsu machine. If using a food processor, pulse in small batches to avoid melting.
While homemade sweet red bean paste offers the best flavor, you can use canned sweet red beans (tong pat) for a quicker preparation. Ensure they are well-drained if too watery.
Patbingsu is best enjoyed immediately after assembly. Prepare all toppings and shave the ice just before serving to maintain its ideal texture.
article: Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice is a beloved Korean Desserts dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
